commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9ae5a9e19161a0dd5c57964b7acd92e6299beb5e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps the cwd-local `.env` check in `shouldLoadCliDotEnv()` with `try/catch` so deleted cwd falls through to state-dir `.env` detection.

PR surface: Source +5. Total +5 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: after the PR's catch, a state-dir `.env` makes `runCli()` call `loadCliDotEnv()`, which still calls `process.cwd()` directly. I did not run a live deleted-cwd CLI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the branch needs a broader loader fix and contributor real-behavior proof from the affected scenario.

target.title: fix(cli): guard shouldLoadCliDotEnv against deleted cwd ENOENT (fixes #73676)
target.author: liuhao1024

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
